hypothesis: E620's Class-B audit flagged src/slm_training/data/quality.py's semantic_contract_for_openui as the strongest remaining candidate for an E618-shaped bug (a regex/heuristic mini-parser misclassifying syntactically-valid current-grammar output) but explicitly left it unverified as live, and named 'confirm whether any current train-data builder still populates record.meta["semantic_contract"] with E613+-era typed-array/nested-object syntax' as its own next step. This iteration answers that directly: is semantic_contract_for_openui's regex mini-parser (_ASSIGNMENT_RE / _DECLARATION_COMPONENT_RE / _IDENTIFIER_RE / _QUOTED_RE) an E618-shape live bug against the current grammar, and does data/quality.py contain any E617-shape (conditionally-populated-state silent no-op) gap in its decode-time-consumed helpers?

method.call_path_tracing: Grepped every caller of semantic_contract_for_openui: src/slm_training/harnesses/experiments/teacher_paraphrase_activation.py:573 (render_canonical_request, builds the actual GENERATE-mode training prompt text via render_semantic_contract_prompt), src/slm_training/harnesses/train_data/pipeline.py:303 (attaches record.meta['semantic_contract'] at build time), src/slm_training/data/edits/__init__.py:502 (emit_transition_records, same pattern for edit-derived GENERATE records), and src/slm_training/data/quality.py:348 (_semantic_contract_reasons, re-derives the contract from record.openui and rejects the record via independent_judge if it drifts from the stored meta). Confirmed no caller in twotower.py, choice_tokenizer.py, or meaningful_program.py -- this function is exclusively a data-build-time / admission-gate path, not a decode-time bias or eval-time scorer, unlike E617/E618/E620's targets.
method.e618_shape_check_multiline_truncation: _ASSIGNMENT_RE = r'(?m)^\s*([a-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*=\s*(.*?)\s*$' only captures a single line per statement (no DOTALL). Hypothesized this would truncate multi-line component trees. Scanned the full on-disk training corpus (src/slm_training/resources/data/train/*/records.jsonl) for the real canonical OpenUI serialization format: every root/binder assignment observed is a single physical line (e.g. 'root = Stack([sep, cap])\nsep = Separator(...)\n...'), confirmed against docs/design/iter-e614/e615/e617's own quoted examples (e.g. 'v0 = ImageGallery([{alt: ":ood.gallery.alt", src: ":ood.gallery.img", details: ":ood.gallery.caption"}])' -- one line). Verified with a live call: semantic_contract_for_openui() on a hand-built multi-line example DOES truncate references to the first physical line of an expression, reproducing the theoretical bug -- but this input shape never occurs in the real grammar's canonical serializer output, so it is not reachable via any current builder.
method.e618_shape_check_key_collision: The reference-detection step strips quoted spans then flags any bare identifier in the unquoted expression that also equals a declared top-level binder name. This misfires only if an object-literal property key (e.g. ImageGallery's src/alt/details) collides with a real top-level binder name in the same program, which would spuriously add a false reference edge. Confirmed via full-schema scan (src/slm_training/dsl/lang_core.py library_schema()) that ImageGallery is the ONLY component in the current grammar with object-literal typed-array items, and its item schema (additionalProperties: false) has exactly three keys: src, alt, details. Scanned the real on-disk training corpus for all 473 unique top-level binder names actually used: none is 'src', 'alt', or 'details'. Traced the binder-name generator (_TypedBuilder._binder, src/slm_training/data/progspec/generate.py:287-290) and confirmed it is structurally incapable of emitting 'src'/'alt'/'details' as a binder name -- it stems only from schema component-type names (openui_prop_order.json's 54 $defs keys, none named Src/Alt/Details), never from property keys. No other synthesis path (teacher paraphrase, edits, frontier description) generates fresh binder names. Collision is structurally prevented, not merely unobserved.
method.e618_shape_check_missed_references: Checked whether a real cross-statement reference (e.g. a variable used as an ImageGallery item's src/alt/details value) could be silently dropped. The schema types src/alt/details as string-only (not a $ref/identifier type), so ImageGallery item values are always quoted literals or :placeholder tokens, never variable references to another top-level statement -- there is no reference to miss inside an object literal in the current grammar.
method.corpus_intersection_check: E620's own 'next' item #1 asked whether any current builder populates record.meta['semantic_contract'] together with E613+-era typed-array/nested-object output. Scanned every records.jsonl under src/slm_training/resources/data/train/ for records where meta.semantic_contract is a dict AND openui contains an ImageGallery object-literal item ('ImageGallery([{', '{src', or '{alt'): zero matches across the entire on-disk corpus. The only corpus with populated semantic_contract meta and non-empty ImageGallery (e297_semantic_contract_judge_v1) always serializes ImageGallery as the empty literal ImageGallery([]) -- pre-dates E613/E614's typed-array-item work.
method.e617_shape_check: Checked whether any data/quality.py helper consumed by decode-time bias code (object_property_matches_slot_role, semantic_role_candidates, slot_compatible_property_names) reads state gated by an orthogonal, easily-unset flag. object_property_matches_slot_role is called from twotower.py's _schema_role_slot_bias (twotower.py:4970-4972) only inside the branch already gated on `weight <= 0.0 or not frames or not slot_contract` (line 4920) -- the exact self._slot_contracts gate E617 fixed and E620 re-verified all readers of. No additional/new gate found; all data/quality.py helpers used at decode time are pure deterministic functions with no internal conditionally-populated state of their own.

finding: Clean. semantic_contract_for_openui has the same regex-mini-parser *shape* as E618's bug (a hand-rolled parser instead of dsl.parser.parse), but every concrete failure mode traced (multi-line truncation, object-key/binder-name collision, missed object-literal references) is either structurally unreachable given the current grammar/schema/synthesis code, or empirically absent from the entire on-disk training corpus (0/N intersection of populated semantic_contract meta with E613+-era object-frame syntax). No E617-shape gap found in data/quality.py's decode-time-consumed helpers; object_property_matches_slot_role is a pure function called only inside twotower.py's already-fixed slot_contract gate.
decision: No code change. This closes out E620's explicitly-scoped-out follow-up (data/quality.py's semantic_contract_for_openui) as audited-and-clean rather than leaving it as an open flag for a future iteration to re-discover. No regression test added -- there is no reachable bug to guard against; the structural-impossibility arguments (schema key set, binder-name generator) are themselves load-bearing on generated code (openui_prop_order.json, progspec/generate.py) already covered by their own existing test suites.
no_metric_changes: No previously reported metric in this lineage changes; no code touched.
next: If a future component adds object-literal typed-array items with property-key names that overlap common binder stems (e.g. a hypothetical component reusing a schema component-type name as a property key), re-run this collision check -- it is currently sound only because the schema's one object-literal component (ImageGallery) has a disjoint key set from all component-type names.

If semantic_contract_for_openui is ever pointed at multi-line OpenUI source (e.g. a future pretty-printed serialization mode), the single-line _ASSIGNMENT_RE truncation bug documented here would become live; worth a DOTALL-aware rewrite or an explicit single-line assertion at that point, not before.

verification.notes: No pytest run added or required: no source file changed. Verification was interactive Python REPL calls against the real semantic_contract_for_openui function and a full scan of the real on-disk training corpus (src/slm_training/resources/data/train/*/records.jsonl), not a hypothesis.

version_stamp.note: No code changed this iteration; version stamp carried over unchanged from the E620 commit (e460f9a) this branch is built on.
